user_prompt: #8  0x400de8b7 in lgfx::v1::i2c::transactionWriteRead(int, int, unsigned char const*, unsigned char, unsigned char*, unsigned int, unsigned long) at .pio/libdeps/thefly_sec0/M5GFX@0.2.20/src/lgfx/v1/platforms/esp32/common.cpp:1840

#9  0x400e3119 in lgfx::v1::i2c::readRegister(int, int, unsigned char, unsigned char*, unsigned int, unsigned long) at .pio/libdeps/thefly_sec0/M5GFX@0.2.20/src/lgfx/v1/platforms/esp32/../common.hpp:193
(inlined by) m5::I2C_Class::readRegister(unsigned char, unsigned char, unsigned char*, unsigned int, unsigned long) const at .pio/libdeps/thefly_sec0/M5Unified/src/utility/I2C_Class.cpp:77
#10 0x400e4d19 in m5::I2C_Device::readRegister(unsigned char, unsigned char*, unsigned int) const at .pio/libdeps/thefly_sec0/M5Unified/src/utility/imu/../I2C_Class.hpp:187
that's new, probably the codec checking and the battery tracker conflicting?
agent_reply: Yep, I think that was it. Not really codec ADC vs battery tracker, but the new codec code was using raw Arduino `Wire` on the same internal I2C bus that `M5.Power.getBatteryVoltage()` uses through M5Unified. The bare `Wire.begin()` in the SGTL5000 enable path was especially suspicious.
I patched it so the codec now shares M5Unified’s I2C path:
- [ExtCodec.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:258) probes with `M5.In_I2C.scanID()` and no longer calls `Wire.begin()`.
- Removed raw `Wire` use from the ExtCodec path.
One note: the builds regenerated `src/version.c`; `tools/test_builds/` is still untracked from before.

commit_message: fixed new i2c code from codec crashing

user_prompt: the SPM1423 is actually on a daughterboard that comes pre-installed in a M5Stack Core2, so if I am adding an external codec, I must remove the SPM1423
right now, please always make sure BCLK is duplicated to GPIO13, GPIO13 never has to be deconfigured
also write out the code to configure the SGTL5000 for 16 KHZ 16 bit stereo
agent_reply: - [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1062) now reasserts GPIO13 as an ESP32 GPIO-matrix mirror of `I2S0O_BCK_OUT_IDX`.
- That mirror is applied after `stop_i2s()`, after NS4168 speaker setup, and after SGTL5000 full-duplex I2S setup, so GPIO13 is not intentionally deconfigured by our code.
- [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1019) now drives the SGTL5000 path with `I2S_MCLK_MULTIPLE_512`, giving 8.192 MHz MCLK for 16 kHz audio.

- [control_sgtl5000.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/control_sgtl5000.cpp:595) now has `configureFor16k16BitStereo()`, which sets SGTL5000 to 16 kHz, 16-bit stereo, slave I2S, 32 Fs BCLK, with ADC->I2S and I2S->DAC routing.
- [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:314) calls that configuration during real SGTL5000 startup.
Validated with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`, which passed. `git diff --check` also passed, aside from the repo’s usual CRLF warnings. I also restored the generated `src/version.c` churn after the build.
- [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1062) now reasserts GPIO13 as an ESP32 GPIO-matrix mirror of `I2S0O_BCK_OUT_IDX`.
- That mirror is applied after `stop_i2s()`, after NS4168 speaker setup, and after SGTL5000 full-duplex I2S setup, so GPIO13 is not intentionally deconfigured by our code.
- [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1019) now drives the SGTL5000 path with `I2S_MCLK_MULTIPLE_512`, giving 8.192 MHz MCLK for 16 kHz audio.

- [control_sgtl5000.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/control_sgtl5000.cpp:595) now has `configureFor16k16BitStereo()`, which sets SGTL5000 to 16 kHz, 16-bit stereo, slave I2S, 32 Fs BCLK, with ADC->I2S and I2S->DAC routing.
- [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:314) calls that configuration during real SGTL5000 startup.
Validated with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`, which passed. `git diff --check` also passed, aside from the repo’s usual CRLF warnings. I also restored the generated `src/version.c` churn after the build.

commit_message: NS4168 works on stereo, added SGTL5000 config for our I2S format

user_prompt: tell me about the I2C writes that should happen when switching the SGTL5000 mic source from the mic input to the line-in input
agent_reply: When this firmware switches SGTL5000 capture from dedicated mic to line-in, the relevant call path is:
That produces these I2C register writes:
Comment says this is `+7.5 dB gain`, about `1.3 Vp-p full scale`.
Bit 2 is the mic/line-in input select in this driver’s model: set means line-in, clear means mic.
So the second write to `0x0020` becomes:
Same value as step 1 right now.

Important bit: switching to line-in does **not** write `CHIP_MIC_CTRL` / `0x002A`. So if the previous mode configured mic bias/preamp with `micGain()`, this code does not explicitly power down mic bias or lower mic preamp settings when leaving mic mode. It just selects line-in via `CHIP_ANA_CTRL bit 2` and sets ADC input level.

Important bit: switching to line-in does **not** write `CHIP_MIC_CTRL` / `0x002A`. So if the previous mode configured mic bias/preamp with `micGain()`, this code does not explicitly power down mic bias or lower mic preamp settings when leaving mic mode. It just selects line-in via `CHIP_ANA_CTRL bit 2` and sets ADC input level.

user_prompt: are you aware if SELECT_ADC will disable the mic bias?
agent_reply: No. `SELECT_ADC` should only select the ADC analog input mux: `0 = Microphone`, `1 = LINEIN`.
In this driver it is bit 2 of `CHIP_ANA_CTRL` / `0x0024`:
// 2 SELECT_ADC Select the ADC input, 0 = Microphone, 1 = LINEIN
Mic bias is controlled separately in `CHIP_MIC_CTRL` / `0x002A`, specifically `BIAS_RESISTOR` bits `9:8`:
// 9:8 BIAS_RESISTOR MIC Bias Output Impedance Adjustment
// If this is set to zero the micbias block is powered off and the output is highZ.
So switching `SELECT_ADC` to line-in does not inherently disable mic bias. In this code, `inputSelect(AUDIO_INPUT_LINEIN)` writes `0x0024` with bit 2 set, but it does not touch `0x002A`.

To actually disable mic bias, you’d need to write `CHIP_MIC_CTRL` with `BIAS_RESISTOR = 0`, while preserving whatever mic gain/bias-voltage bits you care about. Current `micGain()` writes `0x0170 | preamp_gain`, which means `BIAS_RESISTOR = 1`, so mic bias remains enabled after that unless something later clears it.
No. `SELECT_ADC` should only select the ADC analog input mux: `0 = Microphone`, `1 = LINEIN`.
In this driver it is bit 2 of `CHIP_ANA_CTRL` / `0x0024`:
// 2 SELECT_ADC Select the ADC input, 0 = Microphone, 1 = LINEIN
Mic bias is controlled separately in `CHIP_MIC_CTRL` / `0x002A`, specifically `BIAS_RESISTOR` bits `9:8`:
// 9:8 BIAS_RESISTOR MIC Bias Output Impedance Adjustment
// If this is set to zero the micbias block is powered off and the output is highZ.
So switching `SELECT_ADC` to line-in does not inherently disable mic bias. In this code, `inputSelect(AUDIO_INPUT_LINEIN)` writes `0x0024` with bit 2 set, but it does not touch `0x002A`.

To actually disable mic bias, you’d need to write `CHIP_MIC_CTRL` with `BIAS_RESISTOR = 0`, while preserving whatever mic gain/bias-voltage bits you care about. Current `micGain()` writes `0x0170 | preamp_gain`, which means `BIAS_RESISTOR = 1`, so mic bias remains enabled after that unless something later clears it.

user_prompt: what part of the SGTL5000 is initialized during boot and what part is initialized when a recording starts?
agent_reply: - Sets earbud/mic sense ADC pins as inputs.
- Scans for SGTL5000 at the I2C address.
`begin_sgtl5000_control()` then initializes the SGTL5000 register state:
So boot initializes the SGTL5000 power, clocking, digital routing, ADC/DAC routing, initial line-in path, line-in ADC level, mic gain register, headphone volume, and headphone mute.
`g_codec.enable()` itself writes the broad SGTL5000 power/reference/clock/default routing registers:
Then `configureFor16k16BitStereo()` switches it to the project’s 16 kHz, 16-bit stereo I2S config:
Starting a recording file does **not** directly initialize the SGTL5000.
For memo recording, the order is:
`AudioFileRecorder::startRecording()` opens the file, allocates buffers, writes headers, and enables FIFOs. It does not touch SGTL5000 registers.
For external codec hardware, that calls:
- Ensures/configures ESP32 I2S for the external codec via `ensure_exti2scodec_i2s()`.
- Chokes speaker FIFO for mic-only mode.
- Keeps SGTL5000 headphone muted for mic-only mode.
The actual SGTL5000 input selection at recording/mic-mode time is here:

So the short version: boot does the SGTL5000 bring-up and default analog setup; recording start opens the recording first, then entering mic/full-duplex mode reconfigures I2S and refreshes the SGTL5000 analog input route for the current detected/forced codec state.
- Sets earbud/mic sense ADC pins as inputs.
- Scans for SGTL5000 at the I2C address.
`begin_sgtl5000_control()` then initializes the SGTL5000 register state:
So boot initializes the SGTL5000 power, clocking, digital routing, ADC/DAC routing, initial line-in path, line-in ADC level, mic gain register, headphone volume, and headphone mute.
`g_codec.enable()` itself writes the broad SGTL5000 power/reference/clock/default routing registers:
Then `configureFor16k16BitStereo()` switches it to the project’s 16 kHz, 16-bit stereo I2S config:
Starting a recording file does **not** directly initialize the SGTL5000.
For memo recording, the order is:
`AudioFileRecorder::startRecording()` opens the file, allocates buffers, writes headers, and enables FIFOs. It does not touch SGTL5000 registers.
For external codec hardware, that calls:
- Ensures/configures ESP32 I2S for the external codec via `ensure_exti2scodec_i2s()`.
- Chokes speaker FIFO for mic-only mode.
- Keeps SGTL5000 headphone muted for mic-only mode.
The actual SGTL5000 input selection at recording/mic-mode time is here:

So the short version: boot does the SGTL5000 bring-up and default analog setup; recording start opens the recording first, then entering mic/full-duplex mode reconfigures I2S and refreshes the SGTL5000 analog input route for the current detected/forced codec state.

agent_reply: The SGTL5000 mic bias is set whenever `AudioControlSGTL5000::micGain(...)` writes `CHIP_MIC_CTRL` register `0x002A`.
- mic preamp gain bits are currently `0x0` -> `0 dB`, after our recent gain change

Same result: mic bias enabled at `3.00 V` through `2.0 kohm`.

user_prompt: what do I change it to if I have a 2.2 kiloohm bias resistor externally already?
agent_reply: If you already have an external 2.2 kohm mic-bias resistor, you probably want the SGTL5000 internal mic-bias output impedance **powered off / high-Z**.
BIAS_RESISTOR = 0  -> micbias block powered off, output high-Z
BIAS_VOLT     = 7  -> field still says 3.00 V, but bias output is off
That keeps the mic preamp setting behavior but disables the SGTL5000’s internal mic-bias resistor/output.
If you already have an external 2.2 kohm mic-bias resistor, you probably want the SGTL5000 internal mic-bias output impedance **powered off / high-Z**.
BIAS_RESISTOR = 0  -> micbias block powered off, output high-Z
BIAS_VOLT     = 7  -> field still says 3.00 V, but bias output is off
That keeps the mic preamp setting behavior but disables the SGTL5000’s internal mic-bias resistor/output.

user_prompt: where is the ADC gain adjust done in our code when line-in mic is used?
agent_reply: It’s in the SGTL5000 control path, not directly in `AudioManager`.
The line-in mic branch is here: [ExtCodec.cpp](<D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:279>)
2. `lineInLevel(...)` in [control_sgtl5000.cpp](<D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/control_sgtl5000.cpp:713>) writes the final ADC gain value:
So final line-in ADC gain is `left=1`, `right=1`, register value `0x0011`, which is `+1.5 dB` on both ADC channels.

user_prompt: for the line-in-right mic, where is the ADC amplifier gain being defined?
agent_reply: For the line-in-right mic, the SGTL5000 ADC analog gain is defined by `kSGTL5000DefaultLineInLevel` in [conf.h](<D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:98>):
So right now line-in-right gets ADC gain level `1`, same as left, which is `+1.5 dB`.
For the line-in-right mic, the SGTL5000 ADC analog gain is defined by `kSGTL5000DefaultLineInLevel` in [conf.h](<D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:98>):
So right now line-in-right gets ADC gain level `1`, same as left, which is `+1.5 dB`.

user_prompt: Mic bias comes from 3.3V through 2 kilo-ohm of resistance
Preamp is a MAX4466 using a 100 kilo-ohm feedback resistor
Wired to SGTL5000 line-in input, right channel.
SGTL5000 ADC gain set to 9 dB (setting is 6, in 1.5 dB steps)
Noise gate level set to 1.2% of full scale
Mic bias is 2 kilo-ohm internal plus 2.2 kilo-ohm external, driven with 3.0V
Mic amplifier is set to 40 dB gain
Noise gate level set to 3.0% of full scale
does anything here (highlighted in IDE) mismatch our code?
agent_reply: Mostly matches current code. The only mismatch I see is **mic bias resistance wording for the earbud inline mic**.
- Internal mic line-in right: code routes line-in mic as `LineInRight`, and [AudioManager.cpp](<D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1454>) copies only right channel.
- Internal mic SGTL5000 ADC gain: [conf.h](<D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:98>) has `kSGTL5000DefaultLineInLevel = 6`, so 6 × 1.5 dB = 9 dB.
- Internal mic noise gate: [conf.h](<D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:101>) has `kLineInRightSilenceGateThresholdPercentX10 = 12`, so 1.2%.
- Earbud inline mic gain: [conf.h](<D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:99>) has `kSGTL5000DefaultDedicatedMicGainDb = 40`; `micGain(40)` writes the 40 dB preamp setting.
- Earbud inline mic noise gate: [conf.h](<D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:100>) has `30`, so 3.0%.
- The doc says: “Mic bias is 2 kilo-ohm internal plus 2.2 kilo-ohm external, driven with 3.0V.”

- `0x0170` sets `BIAS_RESISTOR = 1`, which according to SGTL5000 bit naming is the internal mic-bias impedance selection, but you should verify from the datasheet/comments whether that is actually **2 kOhm**. The voltage bits in `0x0170` are `BIAS_VOLT = 7`, which is consistent with the high/3.0 V-ish mic bias setting.
So: values match the current code, except I would not state “2 kilo-ohm internal” with confidence unless you’ve verified that `BIAS_RESISTOR=1` equals 2 kOhm for SGTL5000.

The voltage bits in `0x0170` are `BIAS_VOLT = 7`, which is consistent with the high/3.0 V-ish mic bias setting.
So: values match the current code, except I would not state “2 kilo-ohm internal” with confidence unless you’ve verified that `BIAS_RESISTOR=1` equals 2 kOhm for SGTL5000.

user_prompt: 1. Add a small SGTL5000 helper, probably in control_sgtl5000, like configureAdcNotchFilter(1000.0f, 0.8f, 16000).
2. Use PEQ, not the built-in graphic EQ. The SGTL5000 GEQ has a 990 Hz band, but that is broad EQ, not the needle notch we want.
3. Generate one notch biquad with calcBiquad(FILTER_NOTCH, 1000.0f, 0.0f, 0.8f, 524288, 16000, coef).
4. Load it into PEQ filter slot 0, set filter count to 1, select PEQ.
5. When the line-in-right mic path is selected, route ADC -> DAP -> I2S_DOUT via audioPreProcessorEnable().
6. When the dedicated mic path is selected, either leave it enabled or call audioProcessorDisable() depending on desired policy.
7. If hardware notch is enabled, disable our software notch to avoid double filtering.
8. Verify full-duplex playback still works, because DAP is a single block. audioPreProcessorEnable() should still leave DAC fed from I2S_IN, but that needs a capture/playback sanity test.
This is good but I want to avoid modifying `control_sgtl5000.cpp` with such a big change, as it is an externally obtained library
I have added `ENABLE_SOFTWARE_NOTCH_FILTER` to enable/disable the software notch filter
agent_reply: Implemented the SGTL5000 hardware notch path without touching `control_sgtl5000.cpp`.

- Added SGTL5000 ADC notch control in [ExtCodec.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:583>) using the existing public `calcBiquad`, `eqFilter`, `eqSelect`, `eqFilterCount`, `audioPreProcessorEnable`, and `audioProcessorDisable`.
- Line-in-right now loads/enables a 1000 Hz, Q 0.8 PEQ notch and routes `ADC -> DAP -> I2S_DOUT`: [ExtCodec.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:297>).
- Dedicated earbud inline mic now explicitly disables the SGTL5000 audio processor path: [ExtCodec.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:299>).
- `AudioManager` now keeps the software notch disabled during routing to avoid double filtering: [AudioManager.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1205>).
- Moved the software notch compile switch into [conf.h](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:33>) as `//#define ENABLE_SOFTWARE_NOTCH_FILTER`.
- Fixed the disabled-software-notch build path in `MicGainManager`.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly_sec1` passes. Only the existing `fontHeight()` deprecation warning remains.
